DRAPER CITY, UTAH — TITLE IX ZONING REGULATIONS
Representative excerpt covering commercial and industrial districts.
Source: Draper City Code of Ordinances, Title 9.
(This file is a testing fixture. Verify against the live ordinance at
https://library.municode.com/ut/draper/codes/code_of_ordinances)

================================================================================
CHAPTER 9-13. COMMERCIAL DISTRICTS
================================================================================

9-13-010. PURPOSE OF COMMERCIAL DISTRICTS.
The commercial districts established in this title are intended to provide for
a range of retail, service, and commercial uses at scales appropriate to their
locations. Uses that are not expressly listed as permitted or conditional are
prohibited.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9-13-030. COMMERCIAL GENERAL (CG) DISTRICT.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Purpose. The CG District accommodates a full range of retail and service
activities serving community-wide needs, including uses of moderate intensity.

B. Permitted Uses. The following uses are permitted by right in the CG District:
   1. Retail and service establishments, general
   2. Restaurants and food service, including drive-through
   3. Financial institutions and professional offices
   4. Self-storage facilities and mini-warehouse facilities
   5. Wholesale establishments with ancillary retail
   6. Personal service establishments
   7. Commercial recreation, indoor

C. Conditional Uses. The following uses require a conditional use permit in the
CG District:
   1. Automobile service stations and repair
   2. Car wash facilities
   3. Outdoor storage, accessory to a permitted use
   4. Residential uses above ground floor

D. Prohibited Uses. The following uses are expressly prohibited in the CG District:
   1. Heavy manufacturing or industrial uses
   2. Junk yards, salvage operations
   3. Single-family or multi-family residential as a principal use

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9-13-050. COMMUNITY SHOPPING (CS) DISTRICT.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Purpose. The CS District provides neighborhood-scale commercial uses serving
the day-to-day needs of nearby residential areas. Uses are limited to those
compatible with adjacent residential zones.

B. Permitted Uses. The following uses are permitted by right in the CS District:
   1. Retail establishments, neighborhood scale (under 40,000 sq ft)
   2. Personal service establishments
   3. Medical and dental offices
   4. Financial institutions
   5. Restaurants, without drive-through

C. Conditional Uses. The following uses require a conditional use permit in the
CS District:
   1. Self-storage facilities
   2. Mini-warehouse facilities
   3. Drive-through facilities
   4. Automobile service stations

D. Prohibited Uses.
   1. Manufacturing or industrial uses of any kind
   2. Warehousing (except mini-warehouse as a conditional use per subsection C.2)
   3. Adult-oriented businesses

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9-13-070. COMMERCIAL BUSINESS PARK (CBP) DISTRICT.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Purpose. The CBP District provides areas for integrated development combining
office, research, light manufacturing, and supporting commercial uses in an
employment center setting.

B. Permitted Uses. The following uses are permitted by right in the CBP District:
   1. Professional and general offices
   2. Research and development facilities
   3. Light manufacturing and assembly operations
   4. Warehousing and distribution
   5. Self-storage facilities and mini-warehouse facilities
   6. Flex industrial uses (office/warehouse combination)
   7. Ancillary retail and food service (not to exceed 20% of floor area)

C. Conditional Uses.
   1. Automobile sales and fleet operations
   2. Contractor storage yards, screened
   3. Outdoor storage

D. Maximum Building Height: 45 feet.
E. Minimum Landscape: 20% of site area.

================================================================================
CHAPTER 9-14. INDUSTRIAL DISTRICTS
================================================================================

9-14-010. PURPOSE OF INDUSTRIAL DISTRICTS.
Industrial districts are intended to provide locations for manufacturing,
processing, assembling, warehousing, and distribution uses. Industrial uses
shall be sited and operated to minimize impacts on adjacent non-industrial areas.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9-14-020. LIGHT INDUSTRIAL (M1) DISTRICT.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Purpose. The M1 District is established to provide areas for light
manufacturing, assembly, warehousing, distribution, and related industrial uses
that can be located near commercial and residential areas without generating
significant noise, odor, vibration, or other nuisance impacts.

B. Permitted Uses. The following uses are permitted by right in the M1 District:
   1. Manufacturing, light (including assembly and fabrication, limited)
   2. Warehousing and distribution facilities
   3. Self-storage warehouses
   4. Mini-warehouse facilities
   5. Research and development facilities
   6. Flex industrial uses
   7. Wholesale trade establishments
   8. Contractor shops and yards, enclosed
   9. Trucking terminals and freight handling
   10. Public utility facilities

C. Conditional Uses.
   1. Outdoor storage exceeding 10% of lot area
   2. Concrete or asphalt batch plants
   3. Auto wrecking or recycling, fully enclosed

D. Prohibited Uses.
   1. Heavy manufacturing (as defined in Section 9-02-020)
   2. Residential uses of any kind, except caretaker dwelling accessory to the
      primary industrial use
   3. Retail establishments as a principal use (ancillary retail permitted up
      to 15% of floor area)

E. Maximum Building Height: 50 feet.
F. Minimum Lot Area: 20,000 square feet.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9-14-030. MANUFACTURING LIMITED (ML) DISTRICT.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. Purpose. The ML District is similar to M1 but allows somewhat heavier
industrial uses in areas buffered from sensitive receptors.

B. Permitted Uses:
   1. All uses permitted in M1 District
   2. Manufacturing, medium (limited per Section 9-14-030.D)
   3. Building materials supply
   4. Vehicle fleet storage and maintenance

C. Prohibited Uses:
   1. Residential uses of any kind

================================================================================
CHAPTER 9-15. RESIDENTIAL DISTRICTS (Excerpted)
================================================================================

9-15-010. SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL (R-1-10) DISTRICT.

A. Purpose. The R-1-10 District is established for single-family residential use
at a medium density (minimum 10,000 sq ft lots).

B. Permitted Uses:
   1. Single-family dwellings
   2. Home occupations (subject to Section 9-20-040 standards)
   3. Parks and open space
   4. Schools and religious institutions
   5. Family day care (up to 8 children)

C. Prohibited Uses:
   1. Commercial uses of any kind as a principal use
   2. Industrial uses
   3. Warehousing or storage facilities (commercial)
   4. Mini-warehouse or self-storage facilities

9-15-040. MULTI-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL (RM) DISTRICT.

A. Purpose. The RM District provides for multi-family residential development
including apartments, condominiums, and townhomes.

B. Permitted Uses:
   1. Multi-family dwellings
   2. Townhomes and condominiums
   3. Accessory dwelling units

C. Prohibited Uses:
   1. Industrial uses
   2. Commercial uses other than accessory uses to residential
   3. Self-storage or mini-warehouse facilities

================================================================================
CHAPTER 9-16. OPEN SPACE AND PUBLIC FACILITY DISTRICTS (Excerpted)
================================================================================

9-16-010. OPEN SPACE (OS) DISTRICT.
Permitted Uses: parks, trails, natural open space, agricultural uses.
Prohibited: commercial, industrial, residential (except caretaker).
Self-storage, mini-warehouse, and warehousing are prohibited.

9-16-020. PUBLIC FACILITIES (PF) DISTRICT.
Permitted Uses: government buildings, schools, utilities, hospitals.
Self-storage, mini-warehouse, and industrial uses are prohibited.
